item.title: It's another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings, online friends
looks like bug central
sorry everyone
big dog
that's it. moving to Finland
not wrong
the hair is weirding me out more than the clothes
finally, a new liquid
very odd looking, hard to figure out what happened
i love pistachios
mmm hmm
does it mean new zealand folk or actual kiwis
badass alert
animal hoarders are terrible people but the comment made me lol
no need to brag
i'd eat it
it's important
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Man told flooding rescuers he needed to go back to save his "4 children". When they came back they were 4 pups.
item.dc:creator: frocharocha

item.title: Ireland's Eurovision entry, I only heard about them for the first time yesterday and couldn't be more proud, instantly a fan.
item.description: First Irish Eurovision act in my lifetime that i would actually go and see perform live, we have sent so many... lets say poop... acts in the past. Did a double take when i saw just a few second clip last night after they qualified for the finals and had to go watch the full song and look into them. Couldn't believe it was Ireland, 30 years ago they most likely would have been fined or jailed for such a song or performance. Thought it was worth sharing here anyway, im sure others will enjoy :) Sauce me up baby: Artist: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bambie_Thug Video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BNc5zTYkTaQ Screenshot 1: https://www.irishexaminer.com/lifestyle/artsandculture/arid-41389666.html Screenshot 2: https://www.irishexaminer.com/news/munster/arid-41390392.html
item.dc:creator: IRCBrink

item.title: The anniversary of me losing 80 lbs and keeping it gone!
item.description: 28 and 240lbs now 34 and 160! I'm really proud of it. I know selfies aren't allowed. I'm sorry ?
item.dc:creator: Bhart2290

item.title: Bus drivers in Mexico City learning the experience of being passed by a bus while on a bike.
item.dc:creator: SliceOfHairPie

item.title: I saved a guys life, but his leg wasn't attached to his body
item.description: As per the title, the scene was horrific. I spoke to the man and was the person who rang his wife, and they were both so terrified and there was so much blood and the emotional and physical toll is still settling in. So many motorcyclists wear shorts and zoom between cars and are unnecessarily reckless! I'm near having panic attacks every time a bike passes me. My forearms have genuine muscle pain from clenching my steering wheel since I've gone back to work. And ontop of it all, I now have to do a wild goose chase of the one police officer that took my details at the scene, so I can prove I was there to have any chance to have counselling from the incident covered so it won't cost me money from the immediate shock I am experiencing. Saved a guys life, but I'm too poor to even fuel my car up for work next week. I wouldn't change anything, it's not like not saving him would've put fuel in my car, but it sucks that if I need ongoing therapy to make sure I'm okay, I can't actually afford or address that beyond my one session I forced myself to pay for. C'mon universe, make today's phone calls as smooth as possible. (More detail about the accident is in my last two posts) Update: I just had a great talk with the local Sargent of the station near the accident. He is not only going to get a reference number for me for the report, but he's going to send a referral onto trauma counselling provided by the government for accident witnesses as well. He thinks still pursue getting reimbursement for missing work and other doctors appointments relating to it, but he wants to give me all the tools he can. He also offered that at any stage if I need, I can call or pop in and debrief with him, even if it's over a coffee and very informal. Today has definitely felt like a positive step forward.
item.dc:creator: FluroShoes

item.title: Images of Note: Joey "the beast" McRipped
item.description:
Their pronouns? Split/your/lungs.
This is what I want to be doing.
I can't stop laughing.
Nya!
I am awake at 9am and I WHOLE HEARTEDLY AGREE
:c to c:
The fact that a lot of (American) Christians seem to believe this is somehow the one true bible translation as it was "written under a king" is hilarious because when you bump into them you have to be all like "first, go learn Koine, second you know King James was gay as a daffodil right?"
yeah that's basically it.
Do it Gangle, do it for all of us.
:( who did this?
Drake rn
I SHIP IT (http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii)
Emily Scares me http://twitch.tv/abbiistabbii
la creatura!
nyuum!
Caramelldansen is a bop you can kill to.

item.title: Dumpwich Cosmic Horror
item.dc:creator: chicar

item.title: I’m special and rare!
item.description: Today I was diagnosed with Multiple Evanescent White Dot Syndrome. The photo is my retina in my left eye. It is extremely rare especially in males (that’s me!), could possibly be caused by my MS (also me) but people don’t have to have MS to get it - it’s a response to inflammation. It should resolve on its own in up to 10 weeks but they’re prescribing steroids to potentially help. It’s rare enough that they aren’t even sure if the steroids do help. No permanent vision impairment. When the dots go away my vision should return to normal. I’ve been seeing sparkles and shadows in my left eye for a couple weeks and went to a retina specialist today to figure things out. I’m so happy this isn’t permanent! The pictures they took blinded me for a few seconds because they dilated my pupils and then got in real close with a lens and kept flashing super bright lights at me to get a good picture. I asked how rare this is and the doc said she hadn’t seen this in years of practice yet, just when she was in school. She told me my case was extremely interesting.
item.dc:creator: jwillustrat

item.dc:creator: ahorseelbowdeepinme

item.title: You Don't Say: Utah official complains about receiving thousands of fake bathroom snitch form reports
item.description: Dougall said his office has not yet received any complaints that appear to be real. “We haven’t seen anything that we consider legitimate,” he said. Dougall told Utah News Dispatch on Tuesday nearly 10,000 “bogus” reports have been submitted since a new page on his office’s complaint hotline website went live last week. “Most of them are pretty easy to screen out as bogus complaints,” Dougall said during a phone call. “There’s a few hundred that look like they might be more legitimate, but then, you know, facts don’t line up or they put somebody down as a contact, like me or something like that, and we know those are not legitimate.” https://utahnewsdispatch.com/2024/05/07/utah-auditor-transgender-bathroom-law-hoax-complaints/ https://www.lgbtqnation.com/2024/05/utah-official-complains-about-receiving-thousands-of-fake-bathroom-snitch-form-reports/
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: Leon the Professional- Amazing Cinematography
item.dc:creator: fvng

item.title: Yor Forger - SpyxFamily Cosplay
item.description:
I cosplayed as Yor to go to the new "SpyxFamily - Code: White" movie :D It was adorable and I felt adorable in this while watching it. Been utterly obsessed with this anime lately and especially her character, she's so fun.
The movie cup will hold my keys from now on :D It was also fun to find a theatre that played it in Japanese with English subtitles.
Reference image/gif of Yor's outfit for reference
And this gif from the show... because this show is spectacular and everyone should see it :P
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley
